prompts: Length contraction is a phenomenon predicted by the theory of relativity, specifically described by the Lorentz transformation. It occurs when an object is moving at a significant fraction of the speed of light relative to an observer. From the perspective of the observer, the object's length parallel to the direction of motion appears shorter than its proper length, which is the length measured in the object's rest frame. The degree of this contraction depends on the object's speed and is calculated using the Lorentz factor. It's a fundamental aspect of special relativity, showcasing how measurements of space and time are relative to motion.
